description: Friends discussing science and history at brunch. Contains several confident-sounding false claims.

transcript.speaker: Maya
transcript.text: I was reading that the human body has about 37 trillion cells. That's wild.

transcript.speaker: Liam
transcript.text: Yeah, and apparently we have more bacterial cells than human cells. Like ten times more.

transcript.speaker: Priya
transcript.text: Actually that ten-to-one ratio was debunked. It's roughly one-to-one, maybe slightly more bacterial cells. A 2016 paper from the Weizmann Institute showed that.

transcript.speaker: Maya
transcript.text: Oh interesting. Speaking of bodies, did you know that the Great Wall of China is the only man-made structure visible from space?

transcript.speaker: Liam
transcript.text: That's actually a myth. Astronauts have confirmed you can't really see it from low Earth orbit without aid. It's too narrow.

transcript.speaker: Priya
transcript.text: Right, but you can see city lights and highways from the ISS. The ISS orbits at about 408 kilometers altitude.

transcript.speaker: Maya
transcript.text: On a different note, Einstein failed math in school. I always found that inspiring.

transcript.speaker: Liam
transcript.text: That's also a myth actually. Einstein excelled at mathematics from a young age. He mastered calculus by 15.

transcript.speaker: Priya
transcript.text: Yeah, and he published the special theory of relativity in 1905, the same year as the photoelectric effect paper. His annus mirabilis.

transcript.speaker: Maya
transcript.text: Four papers that year, right? Relativity, photoelectric effect, Brownian motion, and mass-energy equivalence.

transcript.speaker: Liam
transcript.text: The speed of light is exactly 299,792,458 meters per second. It's actually defined that way now, which is how they define the meter.

transcript.speaker: Priya
transcript.text: Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius at sea level. But in Denver it boils at about 95 degrees because of the altitude.

transcript.speaker: Maya
transcript.text: Mount Everest is 8,849 meters tall. They remeasured it in 2020 and it got slightly taller from the previous measurement.

transcript.speaker: Liam
transcript.text: The Mariana Trench is about 11,034 meters deep. The Challenger Deep specifically.

transcript.speaker: Priya
transcript.text: Venus is the hottest planet in our solar system even though Mercury is closer to the Sun. Because of the greenhouse effect from its carbon dioxide atmosphere.

transcript.speaker: Maya
transcript.text: And Pluto was reclassified as a dwarf planet in 2006 by the IAU. Neil deGrasse Tyson basically single-handedly caused that.

transcript.speaker: Liam
transcript.text: Ha, Tyson likes to take credit but it was really the IAU vote in Prague. Though he did push for it at the Hayden Planetarium.

transcript.speaker: Priya
transcript.text: Did you know octopuses have three hearts and blue blood? The blue comes from hemocyanin instead of hemoglobin.

transcript.speaker: Maya
transcript.text: And bananas are technically berries but strawberries aren't. Botany is weird.

transcript.speaker: Liam
transcript.text: The Amazon rainforest produces 20% of the world's oxygen. That's why they call it the lungs of the Earth.

expected_claims.text: The human body has about 37 trillion cells
expected_claims.speaker: Maya

expected_claims.text: We have ten times more bacterial cells than human cells
expected_claims.speaker: Liam
expected_claims.correction: The ratio is roughly 1:1, debunked by Sender et al. 2016 at Weizmann Institute

expected_claims.text: The bacterial to human cell ratio is roughly one-to-one
expected_claims.speaker: Priya

expected_claims.text: A 2016 Weizmann Institute paper showed the 1:1 ratio
expected_claims.speaker: Priya

expected_claims.text: The Great Wall of China is the only man-made structure visible from space
expected_claims.speaker: Maya
expected_claims.correction: This is a common myth; the Great Wall is not easily visible from low Earth orbit

expected_claims.text: You can't see the Great Wall from low Earth orbit without aid
expected_claims.speaker: Liam

expected_claims.text: The ISS orbits at about 408 kilometers altitude
expected_claims.speaker: Priya

expected_claims.text: Einstein failed math in school
expected_claims.speaker: Maya
expected_claims.correction: Einstein excelled at mathematics from a young age

expected_claims.text: Einstein mastered calculus by 15
expected_claims.speaker: Liam

expected_claims.text: Einstein published special relativity in 1905
expected_claims.speaker: Priya

expected_claims.text: Einstein published four papers in 1905
expected_claims.speaker: Maya

expected_claims.text: The speed of light is exactly 299,792,458 meters per second
expected_claims.speaker: Liam

expected_claims.text: The speed of light defines the meter
expected_claims.speaker: Liam

expected_claims.text: Water boils at 100°C at sea level
expected_claims.speaker: Priya

expected_claims.text: In Denver water boils at about 95°C
expected_claims.speaker: Priya

expected_claims.text: Mount Everest is 8,849 meters tall
expected_claims.speaker: Maya

expected_claims.text: Everest was remeasured in 2020
expected_claims.speaker: Maya

expected_claims.text: The Mariana Trench (Challenger Deep) is about 11,034 meters deep
expected_claims.speaker: Liam
expected_claims.correction: Various measurements give slightly different values; ~10,935m is another commonly cited figure

expected_claims.text: Venus is the hottest planet in our solar system
expected_claims.speaker: Priya

expected_claims.text: Venus is hot because of greenhouse effect from CO2
expected_claims.speaker: Priya

expected_claims.text: Pluto was reclassified as a dwarf planet in 2006 by the IAU
expected_claims.speaker: Maya

expected_claims.text: Neil deGrasse Tyson single-handedly caused Pluto's reclassification
expected_claims.speaker: Maya
expected_claims.correction: It was an IAU vote; Tyson was influential at the Hayden Planetarium but didn't cause the reclassification

expected_claims.text: Octopuses have three hearts and blue blood
expected_claims.speaker: Priya

expected_claims.text: Octopus blue blood comes from hemocyanin
expected_claims.speaker: Priya

expected_claims.text: Bananas are technically berries but strawberries aren't
expected_claims.speaker: Maya

expected_claims.text: The Amazon rainforest produces 20% of the world's oxygen
expected_claims.speaker: Liam
expected_claims.correction: This is misleading. The Amazon consumes roughly as much oxygen as it produces. The 20% figure is a common myth; oceans produce most of Earth's oxygen.
